MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-26-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
ORDER DENYING DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT AND ENTER JUDGMENT

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CHARLES BOYD AND KELLY BOYD'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT AND ENTER JUDGMENT IS DENIED. A WRITTEN SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT IS NOT ENFORCEABLE UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 664.6, UNLESS IT IS SIGNED BY ALL OF THE PARTIES TO THE AGREEMENT, AND NOT MERELY BY THE PARTIES AGAINST WHOM THE AGREEMENT IS SOUGHT TO BE ENFORCED. (SULLY-MILLER CONTRACTING CO. V. GLEDSON/CASHMAN CONSTRUCTION, INC. (2002) 103 CAL.APP.4TH 30, 37.) SECTION 664.6 REQUIRES THE PARTIES' SIGNATURES, BECAUSE SETTLEMENT IS SUCH A SERIOUS STEP THAT IT REQUIRES THE CLIENT'S KNOWLEDGE AND EXPRESS CONSENT. (IBID.) ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL, ON AUGUST 11, 2015, COMMUNICATED TO THE PARTIES INVOLVED IN THE CASE THAT THE LAST SIGNATURE FOR THE GENERAL RELEASE AND AGREEMENT HAD BEEN RECEIVED, THE COURT DOES NOT HAVE BEFORE IT EVIDENCE THAT ALL OF THE PARTIES, INCLUDING PLAINTIFF, SIGNED THE AGREEMENT. THE COURT CANNOT RULE ON THE MOTION BASED ON THE FACT THAT ALL OF THE PARTIES SIGNED THE PROVISIONAL AGREEMENT BECAUSE IN THE MOVING PAPERS, DEFENDANTS ONLY SOUGHT TO ENFORCE THE GENERAL RELEASE AND AGREEMENT. THE FACT THAT PLAINTIFF SIGNED THE PROVISIONAL AGREEMENT AND THAT AGREEMENT WAS ATTACHED TO THE GENERAL RELEASE AND AGREEMENT DOES NOT MAKE THE GENERAL RELEASE AND AGREEMENT ENFORCEABLE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-02-2016 Vacated

Notice Of Second Motion And Second Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement And Enter Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-20-2016 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-27-2016 Set For Trial On Dec-05-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement And Enter Judgment
Off Calendar Jan-26-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Patrick Campbell of Hollingshead & Associates telephone number 916-630-5049 appeared by telephone for Cross-Defendant Handelin Incorporated dba Original Construction.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CHARLES BOYD AND KELLY BOYD'S Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement And Enter Judgment is denied. A written settlement agreement is not enforceable under Code of Civil Procedure 664.6, unless it is signed by all of the parties to the agreement, and not merely by the parties against whom the agreement is sought to be enforced. (Sully-Miller Contracting Co. v. Gledson/Cashman Construction, Inc. (2002) 103 Cal.App.4th 30, 37.) Section 664.6 requires the parties' signatures, because settlement is such a serious step that it requires the client's knowledge and express consent. (Ibid.) Although Plaintiff's counsel, on August 11, 2015, communicated to the parties involved in the case that the last signature for the General Release and Agreement had been received, the court does not have before it evidence that all of the parties, including Plaintiff, signed the agreement. The court cannot rule on the motion based on the fact that all of the parties signed the Provisional Agreement because in the moving papers, Defendants only sought to enforce the General Release and Agreement. The fact that Plaintiff signed the Provisional Agreement and that agreement was attached to the General Release and Agreement does not make the General Release and Agreement enforceable. Order signed.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement And Enter Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-23-2015 Continued To Jan-26-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. ANGELA J. OKAMURA, ESQ. of Peterson Martin Reynolds LLP, Tel No. 415-399-2900 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Attys. PHILIP H KELLY, ESQ. and JACQUELINE M. MOREIRA, ESQ. of Law Offices of Philip H. Kelly, Tel No. 707-578-7160 appearing for Defendants Charles Boyd and Kelly Boyd;
Atty. PATRICK J. CAMPBELL, ESQ. of Hollingshead & Associates, Tel No. 916-630-5049 appearing telephonically for Cross-Defendant Handelin Incorporated dba Original Construction.

Matter argued by counsel. The Court vacates its tentative ruling and continues the hearing on DEFENDANTS CHARLES BOYD AND KELLY BOYD'S Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement And Enter Judgment to January 26, 2016 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion. The Court grants a continuance to permit a supplemental pleading re: the general release and Mr. Handelin. The Court orders Plaintiff to produce the document in question. Defense counsel will submit a proposed order re: this continuance. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
